prompt:
You are a B2B outreach copywriter. Draft a cold outreach email using the details below. Respond with a JSON object containing exactly two keys: 'subject' (email subject line) and 'body' (email body text). Keep the body under 150 words. Include a specific observation tied to their Series B news, a concrete value proposition, and a low-pressure CTA. No emojis. No fluff.

Template context:
- Sender: Rishav Chatterjee, AI Systems Architect
- Product: soul-outreach — AI-powered B2B email pipeline
- Campaign: VP Engineering outreach — Series B companies

Contact details:
- Name: Marcus Chen
- Title: VP Engineering
- Company: DataFlow Inc
- Industry: Data Infrastructure
- Recent news: DataFlow Inc raised $42M Series B in January 2026 to expand their real-time data pipeline product
- LinkedIn headline: 'Building the data infrastructure layer for the modern enterprise'
- Company size: ~120 employees

Enrichment signals:
- Likely scaling engineering team post-funding
- Probable pain: manual outreach to enterprise prospects at scale
- Hook: Series B often triggers aggressive outbound sales hiring and tooling

Respond with only the JSON object, no explanation.

You are a B2B outreach copywriter. Draft a follow-up email to a contact who has not responded to an initial outreach email sent 10 days ago. Use a different angle — do not repeat the original pitch. Respond with a JSON object containing exactly two keys: 'subject' (email subject line, use Re: prefix) and 'body' (email body text). Keep the body under 150 words. Reference the prior email briefly, offer a new insight or resource, and make the CTA even lower pressure (e.g., a yes/no question). No emojis.

Template context:
- Sender: Rishav Chatterjee, AI Systems Architect
- Product: soul-outreach — AI-powered B2B email pipeline
- Initial email angle: DataFlow's Series B growth and scaling outbound
- Days since initial email: 10

Contact details:
- Name: Marcus Chen
- Title: VP Engineering
- Company: DataFlow Inc
- Original outreach sent: 2026-02-13

New angle for follow-up:
- Share a brief insight: most post-Series-B engineering orgs find their sales-dev toolchain becomes the bottleneck within 6 months of funding
- Offer: a one-page breakdown of how soul-outreach's pipeline handles enrichment + drafting at scale
- CTA: ask if this is even a priority for them right now (yes/no framing)

Respond with only the JSON object, no explanation.

You are a B2B outreach copywriter. Draft a technical cold outreach email targeting a Data Engineering lead. This is a technical peer-to-peer email — not a sales pitch. Tone should be practitioner-to-practitioner. Respond with a JSON object containing exactly two keys: 'subject' (email subject line) and 'body' (email body text). Keep the body under 150 words. Reference a specific technical pain point, mention a concrete implementation detail, and propose a brief technical conversation as the CTA. No emojis. No generic phrases like 'hope this finds you well'.

Template context:
- Sender: Rishav Chatterjee, AI Systems Architect, 6 years data engineering
- Product: soul-outreach — open-source pipeline with structured enrichment + AI drafting
- Campaign: Data Engineering leads — pipeline observability angle

Contact details:
- Name: Priya Nair
- Title: Head of Data Engineering
- Company: Meridian Analytics
- Industry: Business Intelligence / Analytics
- Company size: ~300 employees
- LinkedIn activity: Recently posted about Airflow DAG reliability issues and the cost of silent pipeline failures

Enrichment signals:
- Active pain: pipeline observability and silent failure detection
- Technical stack signals: likely Airflow, dbt, Snowflake or Redshift
- Hook: her LinkedIn post shows active interest in improving monitoring — concrete technical angle available

Respond with only the JSON object, no explanation.
